barb.label: Frenzy Barbarian

strength.detail: Typically 103 for Arreat's Face, or 136 for Stormshield if using one. With Enigma (which adds Str) you can go lower. Don't over-invest — every extra point is wasted.

dexterity.detail: Base dex is fine for most builds. If using swords, you may need ~100-136 for Phase Blades. Axes need almost no dex. Don't invest for max block — Barbs have low base block.

vitality.detail: Dump every remaining point here. With Battle Orders on top, you'll hit 3000-4000+ life. This is your survivability — more life means more leech sustain and more room for error.

energy.detail: Never put points in Energy. Frenzy costs almost nothing, and you sustain mana through leech and Battle Orders. Even early game, mana potions handle it.

barb.notes: Strength and Dex targets depend on your final gear. Plan ahead — check what your endgame weapons and armor require before committing points. You can respec via Akara (once per difficulty) or Token of Absolution.

zon.label: Java Amazon

strength.detail: 156 for Monarch shield (Spirit), or less if using Stormshield (156) or lower-requirement shields. With Enigma or Anni + Torch providing +stats, you can reduce this. Plan around your final gear.

dexterity.detail: If going max block with Stormshield: invest until 75% block (check a block calculator — it depends on level and shield). If using Spirit Monarch, just enough for Titan's (109 dex). Max block is recommended for Hardcore.

vitality.detail: All remaining points go here. Javazons are squishier than Barbs, so every point of life matters. With BO from your Barb partner, you'll still reach 1500-2500 life which is very comfortable.

energy.detail: Tempting early on since Lightning Fury costs mana, but don't do it. Silkweave boots, Spirit shield, and mana leech from Titan's solve all mana issues. Use mana potions in early game.

zon.notes: The Zon's stat allocation is more flexible than the Barb's. Decide early if you're going max block or not — it changes your Dex investment massively. Softcore: skip max block, pump Vita. Hardcore: max block is worth it.

necro.label: Manfred the Necromancer

strength.detail: Typically 156 for a Monarch shield (Spirit) or as low as 58 for Homunculus. With Enigma adding +Str, you can plan lower base strength. Target your final shield choice and stop there.

dexterity.detail: Just enough to equip gear — most summoner necro equipment has no dex requirement. Don't invest for max block; your skeleton army is your defense, not a shield block chance.

vitality.detail: Dump every remaining point here. Summoner necros lack Battle Orders, so raw vitality is your main life pool. With Homunculus or Spirit providing +life and your minions tanking, you'll be comfortable at 1500-2000+ life.

energy.detail: Never invest in Energy. Spirit shield provides +112 mana, and between Spirit, Silkweave boots, and mana potions you'll never run dry. Corpse Explosion costs are manageable with basic mana sustain.

necro.notes: Summoner necros have simple stat allocation. Hit your Strength requirement for Spirit Monarch (156) or Homunculus (58), ignore Dex and Energy, and pour everything into Vitality. If you find Enigma later, the +Str lets you respec and move even more points into Vita.
